Our team understands the unique challenges of treating bed bugs in Graniteville's mix of vintage homes and modern residences, and we offer flexible scheduling and discreet service to minimize disruption to your daily life.
localContext: Graniteville's diverse housing stock, from century-old homes near Willow Brook Park to garden apartments along Forest Avenue, each requires specialized bed bug treatment approaches. We're familiar with the building types throughout the neighborhood, from single-family homes near PS 29 to multi-unit dwellings, and tailor our extermination strategies accordingly.

faqs.question: How much does bed bug treatment cost in Graniteville?
faqs.answer: Bed bug treatment in Graniteville typically ranges from $1,200 to $3,500, depending on the size of your property, the severity of the infestation, and the treatment method selected. We offer free inspections and provide detailed quotes with no hidden fees before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does bed bug treatment take in Graniteville homes?
faqs.answer: Most bed bug treatments in Graniteville are completed in 4-8 hours for heat treatment or require 2-3 visits over several weeks for chemical applications. The exact timeline depends on your home's size and infestation level. Heat treatments offer same-day results, while chemical treatments provide residual protection.

faqs.question: Do I need to leave my Graniteville home during bed bug treatment?
faqs.answer: For heat treatments, you'll need to vacate for 6-8 hours while we raise temperatures to eliminate bed bugs. For chemical treatments, we recommend staying away for 4-6 hours after application. We provide detailed preparation instructions and work around your schedule to minimize inconvenience.

faqs.question: Are bed bug treatments safe for families and pets in Graniteville?
faqs.answer: Yes, all our bed bug treatments are EPA-approved and safe when applied by our certified technicians. We use family-friendly and pet-safe products, and provide clear instructions for re-entry. Heat treatments are completely chemical-free, making them ideal for households with children, pets, or chemical sensitivities.
